mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP expands corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 7.5% signals labor market recovery, boosting consumer spending and equity valuations over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 7.5% in monthly data
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of triggered instances, or t-test p-value exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based demand expansion, which lifts equity prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signal produces negative SPX returns in more than 50% of cases over the evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising inflation environment, sustained increases in the federal funds rate compress equity multiples, yielding negative forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive despite rate hikes during high inflation periods, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative over any 1-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical difference from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll increase exceeding 150,000 jobs signals robust labor demand, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises by more than 150000 in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered months, or the signal fails to predict returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, real rates turn negative, triggering inflation fears that depress equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances meeting the condition, or the interaction term lacks statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment, weak labor data suppresses consumer confidence and equity valuations, producing negative forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 60% of high-unemployment instances, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample tests.
